title: Integrating Graphs, Large Language Models, and Agents: Reasoning and Retrieval
abstract: Generative AI, particularly Large Language Models, increasingly integrates graph-based representations to enhance reasoning, retrieval, and structured decision-making. Despite rapid advances, there remains limited clarity regarding when, why, where, and what types of graph-LLM integrations are most appropriate across applications. This survey provides a concise, structured overview of the design choices underlying the integration of graphs with LLMs. We categorize existing methods based on their purpose (reasoning, retrieval, generation, recommendation), graph modality (knowledge graphs, scene graphs, interaction graphs, causal graphs, dependency graphs), and integration strategies (prompting, augmentation, training, or agent-based use). By mapping representative works across domains such as cybersecurity, healthcare, materials science, finance, robotics, and multimodal environments, we highlight the strengths, limitations, and best-fit scenarios for each technique. This survey aims to offer researchers a practical guide for selecting the most suitable graph-LLM approach depending on task requirements, data characteristics, and reasoning complexity.

title: Can Multimodal Large Language Models Visually Interpret Auditory Brainstem Responses?
abstract: Introduction: Auditory brainstem response (ABR) is a standard objective method for estimating hearing threshold, especially in patients who cannot reliably participate in behavioral audiometry. However, ABR interpretation is usually performed by an expert. This study evaluated whether two general-purpose artificial intelligence (AI) multimodal large language model (LLM) chatbots, ChatGPT and Qwen, can accurately estimate ABR hearing thresholds from ABR waveform images. The accuracy was measured by comparisons with the judgements of 3 expert audiologists. Methods: A total of 500 images each containing several ABR waveforms recorded at different stimulus intensities were analyzed. Three expert audiologists established the reference auditory thresholds based on visual identification of wave V at the lowest stimulus intensity, with the most frequent judgment among the three used as the reference. Each waveform image was independently submitted to ChatGPT (version 5.1) and Qwen (version 3Max) using the same standardized prompt and without additional clinical context. Agreement with the expert thresholds was assessed as mean errors and correlations. Sensitivity and specificity for detecting hearing loss (>20 dB nHL) were also calculated. In cases where the AI and expert thresholds nominally matched, corresponding latency measures were also compared. Results: Auditory thresholds derived from both LLMs correlated strongly with expert opinion, with Pearson r = 0.954 for ChatGPT and r = 0.958 for Qwen. ChatGPT showed a mean error of +5.5 dB and Qwen showed a mean error of -2.7 dB. Exact nominal agreement with expert values was achieved in 34.6% of ChatGPT estimates and 35.6% of Qwen estimates; agreement within +/-10 dB was observed in 75.6% and 80.0% of cases, respectively. For hearing-loss classification, ChatGPT achieved 100% sensitivity but low specificity (20.4%), whereas Qwen showed a more balanced profile with 91.6% sensitivity and 67.5% specificity. Curiously, estimates of wave V latency were markedly poor for both LLMs, with systematic underestimation and weak correlations with the expert judgements. Conclusion: ChatGPT and Qwen demonstrated a moderate ability to estimate ABR thresholds from waveform images, although their performance was not good enough for independent clinical use. Both models captured general patterns of hearing loss severity, but there was systematic bias, limited specificity and sensitivity balance, and poor latency estimation. General-purpose multimodal LLMs may have potential as assistive or preliminary tools, but clinically reliable ABR interpretation will likely require specialized, domain-trained AI systems with expert oversight.

title: HARVEST: Unlocking the Dark Bioactivity Data of Pharmaceutical Patents via Agentic AI
abstract: Pharmaceutical patents contain vast Structure-Activity Relationship tables documenting protein-ligand binding data. While technically public, this information remains computationally inaccessible and effectively dark, trapped in bulky documents that no existing database has systematically captured. We present HARVEST, a multi-agent large language model pipeline that autonomously extracts structured bioactivity records from USPTO patent archives at $0.11 per document. Applied to 164,877 patents, HARVEST produced 3.15 million activity records, recovering 326,342 unique scaffolds and 967 protein targets absent from BindingDB. This pipeline completed in under a week a task that would otherwise require over 55 years of continuous expert labor. Automated extraction achieves 80% agreement with human curated corpus of US patents from BindingDB, a conservative lower bound given identified errors within the reference data. We further introduce H-Bench, a structurally guaranteed held-out benchmark built from this recovered data. Evaluation of the leading open-source model Boltz-2 on H-Bench reveals a two-dimensional generalization gap: performance degrades both on novel scaffolds and on uncharacterized protein targets, exposing fundamental limitations of models trained on existing public repositories.

title: Democratizing Scientific Publishing: A Local, Multi-Agent LLM Framework for Objective Manuscript Editing
abstract: Manuscript preparation is a critical bottleneck in scientific publishing, yet existing AI writing tools require cloud transmission of sensitive content, creating data-confidentiality barriers for clinical researchers. We introduce the Paper Analysis Tool (PAT), a free, multi-agent framework that deploys 31 specialized agents powered by small language models (SLMs) to audit manuscripts across multiple quality dimensions without external data transmission. Applied to three published clinical neurological papers, PAT generated 540 evaluable suggestions. Validation by two expert reviewers (R.B., A.G.) confirmed 391 actionable, high-value revisions (90% agreement), achieving a 72.4% overall usefulness accuracy spanning methodological, statistical, and visual domains. Furthermore, deterministic re-evaluation of 126 agent-suggested rewrite pairs using Phase 0 metrics confirmed text improvement: total word count decreased by 25%, passive voice prevalence dropped sharply from 35% to 5%, average sentence length decreased by 24%, long-sentence fraction fell by 67%, and the Flesch-Kincaid grade improved by 17% . Our validation confirms that systematic, agent-driven pre-submission review drives measurable improvements, successfully converting manuscript optimization from an opaque, manual endeavor into a transparent and rigorous scientific process. Manuscript preparation is a critical bottleneck in scientific publishing, yet existing AI writing tools require cloud transmission of sensitive content, creating data-confidentiality barriers for clinical researchers. We introduce the Paper Analysis Tool (PAT), a free, multi-agent framework that deploys 31 specialized agents powered by small language models (SLMs) to audit manuscripts across multiple quality dimensions without external data transmission. Applied to three published clinical neurological papers, PAT generated 540 evaluable suggestions. Independent validation by two expert reviewers (R.B., A.G.) confirmed 391 actionable, high-value revisions (90% agreement), achieving a 72.4% overall usefulness accuracy spanning methodological, statistical, and visual domains. Furthermore, deterministic re-evaluation of 126 suggested Phase 0 rewrite pairs confirmed text improvement: total word count decreased by 25%, passive voice prevalence dropped sharply from 35% to 5%, average sentence length decreased by 24%, and long-sentence fraction fell by 67%, and the Flesch-Kincaid grade improved modestly. Our validation confirms that systematic, agent-driven pre-submission review drives measurable improvements, successfully converting manuscript optimization from an opaque, manual endeavor into a transparent and rigorous scientific process.

title: Aakhyan: An AI-Powered Vernacular Patient Communication Platform for Oncology in Resource-Limited Settings - System Architecture and Pilot Randomised Trial Protocol
abstract: Inadequate discharge communication is a well-documented contributor to medication non-adherence, missed follow-ups, and preventable readmissions across healthcare systems worldwide. In resource-limited oncology settings, where patients are often low-literate, speak non-dominant languages, and manage complex multi-drug regimens, this problem is acute and largely unaddressed. We present Aakhyan, a vernacular patient communication platform that addresses the full post-discharge arc: from converting English-language discharge summaries into structured, voice-based vernacular explanations, through medication adherence support, to proactive follow-up management - all delivered via WhatsApp. The architecture is novel in its strict separation of concerns: a vision-language model performs structured JSON extraction from discharge images; all patient-facing content is generated deterministically from clinician-approved templates with community-sensitive vocabulary registers. This design eliminates the hallucination risk inherent in generative AI patient communication (documented at 18-82% in prior studies) while preserving the extraction capability of large language models. The platform supports four language registers, Bengali, Hindi, simplified English for tribal populations, and Assamese, with text-to-speech synthesis across all registers, including a custom grapheme-to-phoneme engine developed for Assamese phonology. Beyond discharge communication, the platform includes scheduled medication adherence nudges, interactive follow-up reminders, and a Daily Availability and Patient Notification System (DAPNS) that notifies patients the evening before their follow-up whether their doctor and required investigations are available, preventing wasted trips by rural patients who travel 2-6 hours to reach the centre. A 100-patient stratified randomised controlled study is planned at Silchar Cancer Centre, with structured teach-back assessment at 48-72 hours post-discharge as the primary comprehension outcome and preliminary clinical efficacy as a secondary objective. This paper describes the clinical rationale, technical architecture, safety framework, and positioning of Aakhyan within the existing literature on mHealth patient communication interventions.

title: ChemGraph-XANES: An Agentic Framework for XANES Simulation and Analysis
abstract: Computational X-ray absorption near-edge structure (XANES) is widely used to probe local coordination environments, oxidation states, and electronic structure in chemically complex systems. However, the use of computational XANES at scale is constrained more by workflow complexity than by the underlying simulation method itself. To address this challenge, we present ChemGraph-XANES, an agentic framework for automated XANES simulation and analysis that unifies natural-language task specification, structure acquisition, FDMNES input generation, task-parallel execution, spectral normalization, and provenance-aware data curation. Built on ASE, FDMNES, Parsl, and a LangGraph/LangChain-based tool interface, the framework exposes XANES workflow operations as typed Python tools that can be orchestrated by large language model (LLM) agents. In multi-agent mode, a retrieval-augmented expert agent consults the FDMNES manual to ground parameter selection, while executor agents translate user requests into structured tool calls. We demonstrate documentation-grounded parameter retrieval and show that the same workflow supports both explicit structure-file inputs and chemistry-level natural-language requests. Because independent XANES calculations are naturally task-parallel, the framework is well suited for high-throughput deployment on high-performance computing (HPC) systems, enabling scalable XANES database generation for downstream analysis and machine-learning applications. ChemGraph-XANES thus provides a reproducible and extensible workflow layer for physics-based XANES simulation, spectral curation, and agent-compatible computational spectroscopy.

title: Unsupervised Machine Learning for Adaptive Immune Receptors with immuneML
abstract: Machine learning (ML) enables adaptive immune receptor repertoires (AIRRs) analyses for biomarker identification and therapeutic development. With the majority of AIRR data partially or imperfectly labeled, unsupervised ML is essential for motif discovery, biologically meaningful clustering, and generation of novel receptor sequences. However, no unified framework for unsupervised ML exists in the AIRR field, hindering the assessment of model robustness and generalizability. Here, we present an immuneML release advancing unsupervised ML in the AIRR field through unified clustering workflows, interpretable generative modeling, integration with protein language model embeddings, dimensionality reduction, and visualization. We demonstrate immuneML's utility in three use cases: (i) benchmarking generative models for epitope-specific sequence generation, assessing specificity and novelty, (ii) systematic evaluation of clustering approaches on experimental receptor sequences against biological properties, such as epitope specificity and MHC, and (iii) unsupervised analysis of an experimental AIRR dataset to examine potential confounding, a practice widespread in related fields but unexplored in AIRR analyses.

title: MARCH: Multi-Agent Radiology Clinical Hierarchy for CT Report Generation
abstract: Automated 3D radiology report generation often suffers from clinical hallucinations and a lack of the iterative verification found in human practice. While recent Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have advanced the field, they typically operate as monolithic "black-box" systems without the collaborative oversight characteristic of clinical workflows. To address these challenges, we propose MARCH (Multi-Agent Radiology Clinical Hierarchy), a multi-agent framework that emulates the professional hierarchy of radiology departments and assigns specialized roles to distinct agents. MARCH utilizes a Resident Agent for initial drafting with multi-scale CT feature extraction, multiple Fellow Agents for retrieval-augmented revision, and an Attending Agent that orchestrates an iterative, stance-based consensus discourse to resolve diagnostic discrepancies. On the RadGenome-ChestCT dataset, MARCH significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in both clinical fidelity and linguistic accuracy. Our work demonstrates that modeling human-like organizational structures enhances the reliability of AI in high-stakes medical domains.

title: RAGognizer: Hallucination-Aware Fine-Tuning via Detection Head Integration
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is widely used to augment the input to Large Language Models (LLMs) with external information, such as recent or domain-specific knowledge. Nonetheless, current models still produce closed-domain hallucinations and generate content that is unsupported by the retrieved context. Current detection approaches typically treat hallucination as a post-hoc problem, relying on black-box consistency checks or probes over frozen internal representations. In this work, we demonstrate that hallucination detection based on internal state representation can also serve as a direct training signal. We introduce RAGognize, a dataset of naturally occurring closed-domain hallucinations with token-level annotations, and RAGognizer, a hallucination-aware fine-tuning approach that integrates a lightweight detection head into an LLM, allowing for the joint optimization of language modeling and hallucination detection. This joint objective forces the model to improve the separability of its internal states regarding hallucinations while simultaneously learning to generate well-formed and meaningful responses. Across multiple benchmarks, RAGognizer achieves state-of-the-art token-level hallucination detection while substantially reducing hallucination rates during generation, without degrading language quality or relevance.

title: Faithfulness-Aware Uncertainty Quantification for Fact-Checking the Output of Retrieval Augmented Generation
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) enhanced with retrieval, an approach known as Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), have achieved strong performance in open-domain question answering. However, RAG remains prone to hallucinations: factually incorrect outputs may arise from inaccuracies in the model's internal knowledge and the retrieved context. Existing approaches to mitigating hallucinations often conflate factuality with faithfulness to the retrieved evidence, incorrectly labeling factually correct statements as hallucinations if they are not explicitly supported by the retrieval. In this paper, we introduce FRANQ, a new method for hallucination detection in RAG outputs. FRANQ applies distinct uncertainty quantification (UQ) techniques to estimate factuality, conditioning on whether a statement is faithful to the retrieved context. To evaluate FRANQ and competing UQ methods, we construct a new long-form question answering dataset annotated for both factuality and faithfulness, combining automated labeling with manual validation of challenging cases. Extensive experiments across multiple datasets, tasks, and LLMs show that FRANQ achieves more accurate detection of factual errors in RAG-generated responses compared to existing approaches.

title: VLegal-Bench: Cognitively Grounded Benchmark for Vietnamese Legal Reasoning of Large Language Models
abstract: The rapid advancement of large language models (LLMs) has enabled new possibilities for applying artificial intelligence within the legal domain. Nonetheless, the complexity, hierarchical organization, and frequent revisions of Vietnamese legislation pose considerable challenges for evaluating how well these models interpret and utilize legal knowledge. To address this gap, the Vietnamese Legal Benchmark (VLegal-Bench) is introduced, the first comprehensive benchmark designed to systematically assess LLMs on Vietnamese legal tasks. Informed by Bloom's cognitive taxonomy, VLegal-Bench encompasses multiple levels of legal understanding through tasks designed to reflect practical usage scenarios. The benchmark comprises 10,450 samples generated through a rigorous annotation pipeline, where legal experts label and cross-validate each instance using our annotation system to ensure every sample is grounded in authoritative legal documents and mirrors real-world legal assistant workflows, including general legal questions and answers, retrieval-augmented generation, multi-step reasoning, and scenario-based problem solving tailored to Vietnamese law. By providing a standardized, transparent, and cognitively informed evaluation framework, VLegal-Bench establishes a solid foundation for assessing LLM performance in Vietnamese legal contexts and supports the development of more reliable, interpretable, and ethically aligned AI-assisted legal systems. To facilitate access and reproducibility, we provide a public landing page for this benchmark at https://vilegalbench.cmcai.vn/.

title: Disco-RAG: Discourse-Aware Retrieval-Augmented Generation
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has emerged as an important means of enhancing the performance of large language models (LLMs) in knowledge-intensive tasks. However, most existing RAG strategies treat retrieved passages in a flat and unstructured way, which prevents the model from capturing structural cues and constrains its ability to synthesize knowledge from dispersed evidence across documents. To overcome these limitations, we propose Disco-RAG, a discourse-aware framework that explicitly injects discourse signals into the generation process. Our method constructs intra-chunk discourse trees to capture local hierarchies and builds inter-chunk rhetorical graphs to model cross-passage coherence. These structures are jointly integrated into a planning blueprint that conditions the generation. Experiments on question answering and long-document summarization benchmarks show the efficacy of our approach. Disco-RAG achieves state-of-the-art results on the benchmarks without fine-tuning. These findings underscore the important role of discourse structure in advancing RAG systems.

title: Skill-RAG: Failure-State-Aware Retrieval Augmentation via Hidden-State Probing and Skill Routing
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has emerged as a foundational paradigm for grounding large language models in external knowledge. While adaptive retrieval mechanisms have improved retrieval efficiency, existing approaches treat post-retrieval failure as a signal to retry rather than to diagnose -- leaving the structural causes of query-evidence misalignment unaddressed. We observe that a significant portion of persistent retrieval failures stem not from the absence of relevant evidence but from an alignment gap between the query and the evidence space. We propose Skill-RAG, a failure-aware RAG framework that couples a lightweight hidden-state prober with a prompt-based skill router. The prober gates retrieval at two pipeline stages; upon detecting a failure state, the skill router diagnoses the underlying cause and selects among four retrieval skills -- query rewriting, question decomposition, evidence focusing, and an exit skill for truly irreducible cases -- to correct misalignment before the next generation attempt. Experiments across multiple open-domain QA and complex reasoning benchmarks show that Skill-RAG substantially improves accuracy on hard cases persisting after multi-turn retrieval, with particularly strong gains on out-of-distribution datasets. Representation-space analyses further reveal that the proposed skills occupy structured, separable regions of the failure state space, supporting the view that query-evidence misalignment is a typed rather than monolithic phenomenon.
abstract_zh: 检索增强生成（RAG）已成为大型语言模型利用外部知识的基础范式。虽然自适应检索机制提高了效率，但现有方法仅将检索后的失败视为重试信号而非诊断信号，未能解决查询与证据空间失配的结构性原因。研究发现，大量持续的检索失败并非源于缺乏相关证据，而是源于查询与证据空间之间的对齐间隙。为此，我们提出了 Skill-RAG，这是一种感知失败状态的 RAG 框架，它将轻量级隐藏状态探测器与基于提示的技能路由相结合。该探测器在流水线的两个阶段对检索进行门控；一旦检测到失败状态，技能路由将诊断根本原因，并在四种检索技能（查询重写、问题分解、证据聚焦及针对不可还原情况的退出技能）中进行选择，以在下一次生成尝试前纠正失配。在多个开放域问答和复杂推理基准测试中的实验表明，Skill-RAG 显著提高了多轮检索后仍存在的难题准确率，在分布外（OOD）数据集上增益尤为明显。表征空间分析进一步揭示，所提技能占据了失败状态空间中结构化且可分离的区域，支持了查询-证据失配是“分类型”而非单一现象的观点。

analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
Skill-RAG 提出了一种“失败感知”的检索增强生成框架。其核心逻辑是不再盲目重试检索，而是通过隐藏状态探测器（Hidden-state prober）主动识别检索失败的深层原因，并利用技能路由（Skill Router）针对性地调用四种修复技能（重写、分解、聚焦、退出）来解决查询与证据空间之间的对齐间隙。

## 为什么相关
在病原体鉴定、宏基因组分析或临床微生物学决策中，查询往往涉及复杂的生物学命名或长序列特征，极易与标准知识库产生“空间失配”。Skill-RAG 提供的“问题分解”和“证据聚焦”技能，对于处理复杂的临床微生物学推理任务（如从海量文献中提取特定耐药基因的临床意义）具有直接的架构参考价值，能提升 Agent 在处理专业 OOD（分布外）数据时的鲁棒性。

## 新颖度判断
中高。该研究将 RAG 的失败从“二元状态（成功/失败）”提升到了“类型化诊断状态”。引入隐藏状态探测器来感知模型内部对检索质量的反馈，并将其映射到具体的修复技能空间，这一方法论比传统的自适应 RAG（如仅根据置信度重试）更具解释性和针对性。

## 方法与证据
- **方法**：构建了轻量级隐藏状态探测器和基于 Prompt 的技能路由。探测器在两个阶段进行门控拦截。
- **技能集**：包含查询重写（Query rewriting）、问题分解（Question decomposition）、证据聚焦（Evidence focusing）和退出机制（Exit skill）。
- **实验数据**：在多个开放域 QA 和复杂推理基准上进行测试。
- **结论**：Skill-RAG 显著提升了多轮检索后难题的准确率，尤其在 OOD 数据集上表现强劲。表征空间分析证明了不同失败状态在隐藏空间中是结构化且可分离的。

## 应用价值
对于开发临床微生物学 AI Agent 具有重要意义。在面对罕见病原体或复杂共感染案例时，系统若能识别出“查询与证据不匹配”并自动执行“问题分解”或“证据聚焦”，将极大减少模型产生幻觉的风险。其“退出技能”也能在证据确实不足时及时止损，符合临床诊断的严谨性要求。

## 局限与风险
摘要未说明隐藏状态探测器对不同基础模型（Base LLM）的通用性，也未提及探测器本身的训练成本。在生物医学特定领域，如果缺乏高质量的失败案例标注数据，该探测器的分类准确性可能会受到影响。此外，多轮诊断和技能调用可能会增加推理延迟。

title: CHOP: Chunkwise Context-Preserving Framework for RAG on Multi Documents
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems lose retrieval accuracy when similar documents coexist in the vector database, causing unnecessary information, hallucinations, and factual errors. To alleviate this issue, we propose CHOP, a framework that iteratively evaluates chunk relevance with Large Language Models (LLMs) and progressively reconstructs documents by determining their association with specific topics or query types. CHOP integrates two key components: the CNM-Extractor, which generates compact per-chunk signatures capturing categories, key nouns, and model names, and the Continuity Decision Module, which preserves contextual coherence by deciding whether consecutive chunks belong to the same document flow. By prefixing each chunk with context-aware metadata, CHOP reduces semantic conflicts among similar documents and enhances retriever discrimination. Experiments on benchmark datasets show that CHOP alleviates retrieval confusion and provides a scalable approach for building high-quality knowledge bases, achieving a Top-1 Hit Rate of 90.77% and notable gains in ranking quality metrics.
abstract_zh: 检索增强生成（RAG）系统在向量数据库中存在大量相似文档时，检索准确性会显著下降，导致冗余信息、幻觉及事实错误。为此，我们提出了 CHOP 框架，该框架利用大语言模型（LLM）迭代评估分块相关性，并通过确定分块与特定主题或查询类型的关联来逐步重构文档。CHOP 集成了两个核心组件：CNM-Extractor，用于生成包含类别、关键名词和模型名称的紧凑分块特征（Signatures）；以及连续性决策模块（Continuity Decision Module），通过判断连续分块是否属于同一文档流来保持上下文连贯性。通过为每个分块添加上下文感知元数据前缀，CHOP 减少了相似文档间的语义冲突，增强了检索器的辨别力。在基准数据集上的实验表明，CHOP 有效缓解了检索混淆，实现了 90.77% 的 Top-1 命中率，并在排序质量指标上取得显著提升，为构建高质量知识库提供了可扩展方案。

analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究提出了名为 CHOP 的 RAG 优化框架，旨在解决多文档环境下相似文本导致的检索偏离问题。其核心逻辑是通过 LLM 提取分块的元数据特征（CNM-Extractor）并进行文档流连续性判定，将上下文信息编码进检索单元中，从而提升检索的精确度。

## 为什么相关
在临床微生物学与基因组学领域，研究人员常需处理高度相似的数据，例如不同菌株的耐药性报告、相似病毒亚型的基因序列描述或不同版本的临床指南。传统的 RAG 容易在这些语义重叠的文档中“迷失”。CHOP 提出的上下文保留机制，对于构建病原体鉴定知识库、区分高度相似的临床病例报告具有直接的技术参考价值。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度评分为 6.25（中等偏上）。该方法避开了单纯优化 Embedding 模型的常规路径，转而通过“元数据前缀化”和“文档流重构”来增强分块的语义辨识度。这种将 LLM 引入分块预处理阶段以保留上下文连贯性的思路，在处理结构化程度较低的生物医学文献时具有较强的方法论创新。

## 方法与证据
CHOP 框架包含两个关键组件：
1. **CNM-Extractor**：提取分块的类别、关键名词等特征生成签名。
2. **Continuity Decision Module**：判定连续分块的归属流。
实验证据显示，该方法在基准数据集上达到了 **90.77% 的 Top-1 命中率**。摘要未说明具体使用的基准数据集名称及对比的基准模型（Baseline）细节。

## 应用价值
该框架可直接应用于病原生信分析中的长文本挖掘，如从海量文献中精准检索特定病原体的致病机制或耐药位点。其“上下文感知元数据”能有效减少因分块截断导致的语义丢失，提升 AI Agent 在复杂临床决策支持任务中的事实准确性。

## 局限与风险
摘要中提到的“迭代评估分块相关性”可能带来显著的计算开销和推理延迟，这在需要快速响应的临床微生物鉴定场景中可能成为瓶颈。此外，CNM-Extractor 对生物医学专业术语（如复杂的分类学命名或基因代号）的提取准确率在摘要中未予说明，可能存在领域迁移风险。

title: Whose Facts Win? LLM Source Preferences under Knowledge Conflicts
abstract: As large language models (LLMs) are more frequently used in retrieval-augmented generation pipelines, it is increasingly relevant to study their behavior under knowledge conflicts. Thus far, the role of the source of the retrieved information has gone unexamined. We address this gap with a novel framework to investigate how source preferences affect LLM resolution of inter-context knowledge conflicts in English, motivated by interdisciplinary research on credibility. By using synthetic sources, we study preferences for different types of sources without inheriting the biases of specific real-world sources. With a comprehensive, tightly-controlled evaluation of 13 open-weight LLMs, we find that LLMs prefer institutionally-corroborated information (e.g., government or newspaper sources) over information from people and social media. However, these source preferences can be reversed by simply repeating information from less credible sources. To mitigate repetition effects and maintain consistent preferences, we propose a novel method that reduces repetition bias by up to 79.2%, while also maintaining at least 72.5% of original preferences. We release all data and code to encourage future work on credibility and source preferences in knowledge-intensive NLP.
abstract_zh: 随着大语言模型（LLM）在检索增强生成（RAG）流程中的应用日益广泛，研究其在知识冲突下的行为变得至关重要。目前，检索信息的来源对模型决策的影响尚不明确。本研究提出了一个新颖框架，旨在调查来源偏好如何影响 LLM 在英文环境下的上下文知识冲突解决。通过使用合成来源，研究排除了特定现实来源的固有偏见，对 13 个开源 LLM 进行了严格受控的评估。结果显示，LLM 倾向于选择经过机构证实的来源（如政府或报纸），而非个人或社交媒体。然而，这种偏好极易受重复效应影响：简单重复低可信度来源的信息即可扭转模型选择。为缓解此问题，研究提出了一种新方法，可将重复偏差降低高达 79.2%，同时保留至少 72.5% 的原始来源偏好。本研究发布了所有数据和代码，以推动知识密集型 NLP 领域中关于可信度和来源偏好的后续研究。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更像方法创新，可能直接关联 metagenomics、long-read 或 pathogen identification 流程优化。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究探讨了 LLM 在面对检索到的冲突信息时，如何根据“信息来源”的可信度进行抉择。研究发现 LLM 存在明显的来源偏好（偏向机构而非个人），但这种偏好会被信息的简单重复所干扰（重复偏差）。研究提出了一种算法来纠正这种偏差，确保模型能更稳定地依据来源可信度做出判断。

## 为什么相关
在病原体鉴定、临床微生物学或基因组学研究中，LLM Agent 经常需要处理来自不同数据库（如 NCBI、GISAID 或本地临床报告）的冲突数据。例如，当一份政府公共卫生报告与社交媒体上的零散病例描述在耐药性结论上不一致时，模型能否正确识别并优先采用高权威性来源（机构证实）的信息，直接关系到临床决策的安全性。本文提出的缓解重复偏差的方法，对于构建可靠的医学知识问答系统具有直接参考价值。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度较高（5.25）。该研究首次系统性地量化了“来源身份”在 LLM 知识冲突解决中的权重，并创新性地使用了“合成来源”框架来剥离模型预训练阶段带入的现实世界偏见。其针对“重复效应”对来源偏好逆转的研究，揭示了 RAG 系统中一个此前被忽视的鲁棒性漏洞。

## 方法与证据
- **实验对象**：13 个开源大语言模型（open-weight LLMs）。
- **评估框架**：构建了一个受控的合成来源框架，模拟政府、报纸、个人和社交媒体四类来源。
- **关键指标**：研究发现 LLM 初始偏好机构来源，但重复低信度信息会扭转这一偏好。提出的新方法将重复偏差（repetition bias）降低了 79.2%，同时保留了 72.5% 的原始来源偏好。
- **数据规模**：摘要未说明具体的 prompt 数量，但强调是“全面且严格受控”的评估。

## 应用价值
对于开发病原体生物信息学 Agent 的开发者而言，该研究提供了一种优化 RAG 流程的思路：在处理多源异构数据（如文献、临床指南、测序报告）时，不仅要考虑语义相关性，还需通过算法干预来强化对权威来源（如 CDC、WHO 官方数据）的权重，并防止因某些错误信息在检索结果中多次出现而导致的“三人成虎”效应。

## 局限与风险
- **语言局限**：研究仅针对英文环境（in English），在中文临床语境下的表现尚未验证。
- **领域泛化**：实验使用的是合成来源，虽然能排除偏见，但可能无法完全模拟医学领域中极其复杂的权威等级（如不同影响因子的期刊、不同级别的专家共识）。
- **模型范围**：仅测试了开源模型，闭源模型（如 GPT-4）的表现摘要未提及。

title: Evaluating Large Language Models for Transparent Quality-of-Care Measurement in Children with ADHD
abstract: ImportanceGuideline-concordant care for young children with attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) includes recommending parent training in behavior management (PTBM) as first-line treatment. However, assessing guideline adherence through manual chart review is time-consuming and costly, limiting scalable and timely quality-of-care measurement. ObjectiveTo evaluate the accuracy and explainability of large language models (LLMs) in identifying PTBM recommendations in pediatric electronic health record (EHR) notes as a scalable alternative to manual chart review. Design, Setting, and ParticipantsThis retrospective cohort study was conducted in a community-based pediatric healthcare network in California consisting of 27 primary care clinics. The study cohort included children aged 4-6 years with [≥] 2 primary care visits between 2020-2024 and ICD-10 diagnoses of ADHD or ADHD symptoms (n=542 patients). Clinical notes from the first ADHD-related visit were included. A stratified subset of 122 notes, including all cases with model disagreement, was manually annotated to assess model performance in identifying PTBM recommendations and rank model explanations. ExposuresAssessment and plan sections of clinical notes were analyzed using three generative large language models (Claude-3.5, GPT-4o, and LLaMA-3.3-70B) to identify the presence of PTBM recommendations and generate explanatory rationales and documentation evidence. Main Outcomes and MeasuresModel performance in identifying PTBM recommendations (measured by sensitivity, positive predictive value (PPV), and F1-score) and qualitative explainability ratings of model-generated rationales (based on the QUEST framework). ResultsAll three models demonstrated high performance compared to expert chart review. Claude-3.5 showed balanced performance (sensitivity=0.89, PPV=0.95, and F1-score=0.92) and ranked highest in explainability. LLaMA3.3-70B achieved sensitivity=0.91, PPV=0.89, and F1-score=0.90, ranking second for explainability. GPT-4o had the highest PPV [0.97] but lowest sensitivity [0.82], with an F1-score of 0.89 and the lowest explainability ranking. Based on classifications from the best-performing model, Claude-3.5, 26.4% (143/542) of patients had documented PTBM recommendations at their first ADHD-related visit. Conclusions and RelevanceLLMs can accurately extract guideline-concordant clinician recommendations for non-pharmacological ADHD treatment from unstructured clinical notes while providing clear explanations and supporting evidence. Evaluating model explainability as part of LLM implementation for medical chart review tasks can promote transparent and scalable solutions for quality-of-care measurement.
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更接近临床/监测落地，适合评估其对快速识别、预警或治疗辅助的实际价值。

title: PathwaySeeker: Evidence-Grounded AI Reasoning over Organism-Specific Metabolic Networks
abstract: Metabolic activity is not an intrinsic property of an organism, but an emergent state shaped by environmental and experimental context. Despite recent advances in large language models (LLMs) and multi-omics profiling, current computational frameworks struggle to represent and reason over metabolism in a condition-specific manner. General-purpose AI systems operate on static, public biochemical knowledge, while multi-omics datasets capture dynamic measurements without a structured framework for mechanistic interpretation. As a result, metabolic networks remains analysis remains disconnected from the experimental states that define biological function. Here, we introduce PathwaySeeker, an evidence-grounded AI system for organism-specific metabolic network reasoning. PathwaySeeker reconstructs sample-specific metabolic graphs from integrated proteomic and metabolomic data, fine-tunes an LLM on the resulting graph structure, and verifies each reasoning step against the experimental graph through iterative hypothesis search, an approach we term Oracle-in-the-Loop inference. Every output claim carries explicit evidence provenance, distinguishing experimentally confirmed relationships from biochemically plausible hypotheses requiring validation. We demonstrate the system using multi-omics data from the non-model white-rot fungus Trametes versicolor, where PathwaySeeker recovers branched phenylpropanoid pathways and transparently stratifies confirmed reactions from testable extensions. Post-hoc thermodynamic analysis condition-specific metabolite dynamics support the biological feasibility of the reconstructed routes. By embedding experimental evidence provenance directly into language model-guided metabolic network reasoning, PathwaySeeker enables systematic differentiation between experimentally grounded knowledge and structured hypothesis, bridging frontier AI capabilities with organism-specific experimental evidence.

title: Training-Free Cross-Lingual Dysarthria Severity Assessment via Phonological Subspace Analysis in Self-Supervised Speech Representations
abstract: Dysarthric speech severity assessment typically requires either trained clinicians or supervised machine learning models built from labelled pathological speech data, limiting scalability across languages and clinical settings. We present a training-free method (no supervised severity model is trained; feature directions are estimated from healthy control speech using a pretrained forced aligner) that quantifies dysarthria severity by measuring the degradation of phonological feature subspaces within frozen HuBERT representations. For each speaker, we extract phone-level embeddings via Montreal Forced Aligner, compute d scores along phonological contrast directions (nasality, voicing, stridency, sonorance, manner, and four vowel features) derived exclusively from healthy control speech, and construct a 12-dimensional phonological profile. Evaluating 890 speakers across10corpora, 5 languages for the full MFA pipeline (English, Spanish, Dutch, Mandarin, French) and 3 primary aetiologies (Parkinsons disease, cerebral palsy, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis), we find that all five consonant d features correlate significantly with clinical severity (random-effects meta-analysis rho = -0.50 to -0.56, p < 2 x 10^-4; pooled Spearman rho = -0.47 to -0.55 with bootstrap 95% CIs not crossing zero), with the effect replicating within individual corpora, surviving FDR correction, and remaining robust to leave-one-corpus-out removal and alignment quality controls. Nasality d decreases monotonically from control to severe in 6 of 7 severity-graded corpora. Mann-Whitney U tests confirm that all 12 features distinguish controls from severely dysarthric speakers (p < 0.001).The method requires no dysarthric training data and applies to any language with an existing MFA acoustic model (currently 29 languages) or a model trained from healthy speech alone. It produces clinically interpretable per-feature profiles. We release the full pipeline and phone feature configurations for six languages to support replication and clinical adoption. Author SummaryOne of the authors has lived with ALS for sixteen years. Bernard Muller, who built this entire analytical pipeline using only eye-tracking technology, has experienced the progression of the disease firsthand, including the dysarthric speech that comes with advancing ALS and the tracheostomy that followed. The problem this paper addresses is not abstract to him, and that shapes how the method was designed. We developed a method to measure how well a person with dysarthria can produce distinct speech sounds, without needing any recordings of disordered speech for training. Our approach works by analysing how a widely available AI speech model organises different sound categories -- such as nasal versus oral consonants, or voiced versus voiceless sounds -- and measuring whether those categories become harder to tell apart. We tested this on 890 speakers across 10 datasets in five languages, covering Parkinsons disease, cerebral palsy, and ALS. Because the method only needs healthy speech recordings to set up, it applies to any language with an existing acoustic model, currently covering 29 languages. The resulting profiles show clinicians which specific aspects of speech production are degrading, rather than providing a single opaque severity score. This could support remote monitoring of speech decline in neurodegenerative disease and enable screening in languages and settings where specialist assessment is unavailable.

title: Simulated Reasoning and Self-Verification for Psychiatric Diagnosis in Generalist Large Language Models: Comparative Evaluation
abstract: BackgroundLarge language models (LLMs), and, more recently, large reasoning models (LRMs) have rapidly garnered significant interest for application in psychiatry and behavioral health. However, recent studies have identified significant shortcomings and potential risks in the performance of LLM-based systems, complicating their application to psychiatric diagnosis. Two promising approaches to addressing these challenges and improving the efficacy of these models are simulated reasoning (SR) and self-verification (SV), in which additional "reasoning tokens" are used to guide model output, either during or after inference. ObjectivesWe aimed to explore how the use of SR (via LRMs) and SV (via supplemental prompting) affect the psychiatric diagnostic performance of LLMs. Methods106 case vignettes and associated diagnoses were extracted from the DSM-5-TR Clinical Cases book, with permission. Both an LLM and LRM model were selected from the latest available model generation for each of the two vendors studied (OpenAI and Google). Two inference approaches were developed, a Basic approach that directly prompted models to provide diagnoses, and a SV approach that augmented the Basic approach with additional prompts. All case vignettes were processed by the two LLMs, two LRMs, and two inference approaches, and diagnostic performance was evaluated using the sensitivity and positive predictive value (PPV). Binomial generalized linear mixed models were used to test for significant differences between the model vendors (OpenAI, Google), type (LLM, LRM), and addition of an SV prompt. ResultsAll vignettes were successfully processed by each model and inference approach. Sensitivity ranged from 0.732 to 0.817, and PPV ranged from 0.534 to 0.779. The best overall performance was found in the o3-pro LRM using SV, with a sensitivity of 0.782 and a PPV of 0.779. No statistically significant fixed effects were found for sensitivity. For PPV, a statistically significant effect was found for prompt type (SV, p=0.007), model type (LRM, p=0.009). No significant interaction effects were identified. ConclusionsWe found that both SR and SV yielded statistically significant improvements in the PPV, without significant differences in the sensitivity. The addition of the manually specified SV prompt improved the PPV even when simulated reasoning was used. This suggests that future efforts to apply language models in behavioral health could benefit from manually crafted reasoning prompts and automated SR.

title: A Conversational Artificial Intelligence Framework for Comparative Pathway-Level Profiling of Sezary Syndrome and Primary Cutaneous CD8+ Aggressive Epidermotropic Cytotoxic T-Cell Lymphoma (PCAECTCL)
abstract: Background: Sezary syndrome (SS) is an aggressive leukemic variant of cutaneous T-cell lymphoma (CTCL) with distinct clinical and biological features compared to rarer entities such as primary cutaneous CD8+ aggressive epidermotropic cytotoxic T-cell lymphoma (PCAECTCL). Although recurrent genomic alterations in CTCL have been described, comparative analyses at the pathway level across biologically divergent subtypes remain limited. Here, we leveraged a conversational artificial intelligence (AI) platform for precision oncology to enable rapid, integrative, and hypothesis-driven interrogation of publicly available genomic datasets. Methods: We conducted a secondary analysis of somatic mutation and clinical data from the Columbia University CTCL cohort accessed via cBioPortal. Cases were stratified into SS (n=26) and PCAECTCL (n=13). High-confidence coding variants were curated and mapped to biologically relevant signaling pathways and functional gene categories implicated in CTCL pathogenesis. Pathway-level mutation frequencies were compared using Chi-square or Fisher's exact tests, with effect sizes quantified as odds ratios. Tumor mutational burden (TMB) was compared using the Wilcoxon rank-sum test. Subtype-specific co-mutation patterns were evaluated using pairwise association analyses and visualized through oncoplots and network heatmaps. Conversational AI agents, AI-HOPE, were used to iteratively refine cohort definitions, prioritize pathway-level signals, and contextualize findings. Results: TMB was comparable between SS and PCAECTCL (p = 0.96), indicating no significant difference in global mutational load. In contrast, pathway-centric analyses revealed marked qualitative differences. SS demonstrated enrichment of alterations in epigenetic regulators, tumor suppressor and cell-cycle control pathways, NFAT signaling, and DNA damage response mechanisms, consistent with transcriptional dysregulation and immune modulation. PCAECTCL exhibited relatively higher frequencies of alterations involving epigenetic regulators and MAPK pathway signaling, suggesting distinct oncogenic dependencies. Co-mutation analysis revealed a more constrained and focused interaction landscape in SS, whereas PCAECTCL displayed broader and more heterogeneous co-mutation networks, indicative of divergent evolutionary trajectories. Notably, ERBB2 mutations were significantly enriched between subtypes (p = 0.031), highlighting a potential subtype-specific therapeutic vulnerability. Conclusions: This study demonstrates that SS is distinguished from PCAECTCL not by increased mutational burden but by distinct pathway-level architectures, particularly involving epigenetic regulation, immune signaling, and transcriptional control. These findings generate biologically grounded, testable hypotheses for subtype-specific therapeutic targeting and underscore the value of conversational AI as a scalable framework for accelerating discovery in translational cancer genomics.
abstract_zh: 本研究利用对话式人工智能（AI）框架对塞扎里综合征（SS）与原发性皮肤CD8+侵袭性亲表皮细胞毒性T细胞淋巴瘤（PCAECTCL）进行通路水平的比较分析。研究基于哥伦比亚大学CTCL队列（包含26例SS和13例PCAECTCL）的体细胞突变数据，采用AI-HOPE对话智能体进行队列定义优化、通路信号优先级排序及结果背景化分析。结果显示，SS与PCAECTCL的肿瘤突变负荷（TMB）无显著差异（p=0.96），但在通路架构上存在显著定性区别。SS显著富集了表观遗传调节、肿瘤抑制、NFAT信号及DNA损伤修复通路的改变；而PCAECTCL在表观遗传调节和MAPK通路中表现出更高的突变频率。共突变分析显示SS的相互作用景观更为集中，而PCAECTCL则表现出更广泛的异质性。此外，ERBB2突变在亚型间存在显著差异（p=0.031），提示了潜在的治疗靶点。研究表明，对话式AI可作为加速转化癌症基因组学发现的可扩展框架。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更像方法创新，可能直接关联 metagenomics、long-read 或 pathogen identification 流程优化。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究通过名为“AI-HOPE”的对话式人工智能框架，对两种皮肤T细胞淋巴瘤（SS和PCAECTCL）的基因组数据进行了深度挖掘。研究核心在于超越单纯的突变频率对比，利用AI辅助进行通路水平的富集分析和共突变网络构建，揭示了两种疾病在表观遗传和信号传导通路上的本质差异。

## 为什么相关
本研究涉及基因组学与AI/LLM的交叉。虽然研究对象是淋巴瘤，但其提出的“对话式AI驱动的基因组分析框架”直接关联到病原生信和临床微生物学中的复杂组学数据解释流程，特别是如何利用LLM Agent优化假设生成和数据注释，对病原体演化分析具有方法论参考价值。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度中等偏上（0.75）。其创新点不在于开发了新的生物信息学算法，而在于引入了“AI-HOPE”对话智能体作为研究流程的中间层，将传统的生信分析（如cBioPortal数据提取、统计检验）与LLM的逻辑推理能力结合，实现了“假设驱动”的自动化组学分析。

## 方法与证据
- **数据规模**：分析了哥伦比亚大学队列中的39个样本（26例SS，13例PCAECTCL）。
- **AI工具**：使用了名为 AI-HOPE 的对话式AI智能体，用于迭代完善队列定义和优先化通路信号。
- **统计证据**：通过Wilcoxon秩和检验确认TMB无显著差异（p=0.96）；通过Fisher精确检验发现ERBB2突变在亚型间具有显著富集特征（p=0.031）。
- **可视化**：利用癌基因图谱（Oncoplots）和网络热图展示了SS集中的共突变模式与PCAECTCL异质性网络的对比。

## 应用价值
该研究展示了AI Agent在精准医学中的落地场景：不仅能识别出ERBB2等潜在治疗靶点，还能通过自动化流程缩短从原始基因组数据到生物学结论的转化时间。这种框架可被移植到临床微生物基因组分析中，用于快速识别耐药通路或毒力因子的演化规律。

## 局限与风险
- **样本量限制**：总样本量仅39例，对于构建复杂的共突变网络而言规模较小，结论需在大规模队列中验证。
- **AI角色定位**：摘要显示AI主要用于“优化、排序和背景化”，属于弱AI辅助分析，而非直接从原始序列中学习特征的强AI模型。
- **数据源单一**：仅依赖cBioPortal的次级分析，缺乏多组学（如转录组、蛋白质组）的交叉验证。

title: Discovery of the rosalexin pathway expands the modular network of maize diterpenoid chemical defenses.
abstract: The evolutionary expansion of specialized metabolism has shaped the ability of plants to adapt to combined pathogen, pest, and other environmental pressures. For instance, the duplication and divergence of ancestral gibberellin pathway genes have given rise to specialized kauralexin and dolabralexin diterpenoids in maize (Zea mays) that serve as core components of disease resistance and stress adaptation. Here, we describe the biosynthesis and elicited production of rosalexins as a previously unrecognized component of the maize chemical defense network. By integrating genomics-enabled gene discovery, combinatorial enzyme assays, and AI-assisted enzyme mechanistic studies we show that maize rosalexin biosynthesis proceeds via a distinct 5-rosanol scaffold formed by the pairwise activity of two diterpene synthases, ZmTPS38/CPS2/AN2 and ZmTPS42/KSL1, recruited from gibberellin metabolism. Further oxygenation by the promiscuous P450 enzyme, ZmCYP71Z18, yields epoxyrosanol that, in turn, can undergo epoxide ring opening to form trihydroxyrosanol. Epoxyrosanol, but not 5-rosanol or trihydroxyrosanol, display strong inhibitory activity on fungal pathogen growth in vitro, highlighting the contribution of the epoxide group to antibiotic efficacy. Large variation in rosalexin presence and abundance exists across maize genotypes due to expansive ZmTPS42/KSL1 gene sequence variation and pseudogenization. Transcriptomics and targeted metabolomics demonstrated the pathogen-elicited accumulation of rosalexins in maize lines featuring functional ZmTPS42/KSL1 genes. However, no dominant pathogen resistance phenotype was observed in association with rosalexin abundance. These collective findings expand our knowledge of how multiple interconnected diterpenoid pathways arose in maize via duplication of hormone-metabolic genes and enable the utilization of a common precursor to form modular chemical defense layers.
abstract_zh: 植物特化代谢的演化增强了其应对病原体和环境压力的能力。在玉米中，祖先赤霉素途径基因的重复与分化产生了高良姜素和多拉布拉素等二萜类化合物，构成了抗病性的核心。本研究通过整合基因组学基因挖掘、组合酶活性测定及 AI 辅助的酶机制研究，揭示了玉米化学防御网络中此前未被发现的新成分——rosalexins 的生物合成途径。研究证明，rosalexin 的合成始于由赤霉素代谢招募的两种二萜合酶 ZmTPS38 和 ZmTPS42 形成的 5-rosanol 支架。随后，多功能 P450 酶 ZmCYP71Z18 将其氧化为具有抗菌活性的 epoxyrosanol。体外实验证实，epoxyrosanol 对真菌病原体具有强抑制作用，而其衍生物则无此活性。尽管转录组和代谢组分析显示病原体可诱导 rosalexin 积累，但由于 ZmTPS42 的基因变异和假基因化，该物质在不同基因型中分布差异巨大，且未观察到显着的显性抗病表型。该发现扩展了对玉米模块化化学防御层演化的认知。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更像方法创新，可能直接关联 metagenomics、long-read 或 pathogen identification 流程优化。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究发现了玉米中一种新型二萜类化学防御物质 rosalexins 及其生物合成途径。研究阐明了从赤霉素代谢途径招募的基因（ZmTPS38、ZmTPS42 和 ZmCYP71Z18）如何通过模块化组合，合成具有抗真菌活性的 epoxyrosanol。同时，研究揭示了该途径在不同玉米品种中的遗传多样性及其受病原体诱导的表达特征。

## 为什么相关
该研究属于植物病原基因组学（Pathogenomics）范畴，重点关注宿主如何通过特化代谢产物抵御病原体侵害。对于理解植物-微生物互作中的化学防御机制具有重要意义，特别是揭示了代谢途径如何通过基因重复和功能分化来应对生物胁迫。

## 新颖度判断
**中等偏高**。虽然玉米二萜类代谢已有较多研究，但 rosalexin 作为一个“此前未被识别”的防御成分被首次完整报道。其新颖性在于揭示了 5-rosanol 这一独特支架的形成机制，以及环氧基团（epoxide group）对抗菌功效的关键贡献。

## 方法与证据
1. **基因挖掘与验证**：利用基因组学手段识别候选基因，并通过组合酶活性测定（combinatorial enzyme assays）验证了 ZmTPS38 和 ZmTPS42 的催化功能。
2. **AI 辅助研究**：摘要提到使用“AI-assisted enzyme mechanistic studies”（AI 辅助酶机制研究）来解析酶的催化机理，这属于 AI 在结构生物学或生物化学领域的交叉应用。
3. **表型与活性证据**：通过体外（in vitro）实验对比了 epoxyrosanol、5-rosanol 和 trihydroxyrosanol 对真菌生长的抑制率；利用转录组学和靶向代谢组学证明了病原体诱导的表达模式。

## 应用价值
1. **作物育种**：识别了 ZmTPS42/KSL1 基因的序列变异和假基因化，可作为分子标记用于筛选或培育具有更强化学防御能力的玉米品种。
2. **生物农药开发**：epoxyrosanol 的强效抗真菌活性为开发新型天然抗生素提供了分子模板。

## 局限与风险
1. **AI 交叉程度**：属于**弱 AI 交叉**。AI 仅作为辅助工具用于酶机制研究，并非研究的核心驱动力或方法论创新。
2. **表型不一致**：尽管 rosalexin 在体外有活性且受病原体诱导，但在实验中“未观察到显着的显性抗病表型”，说明其在复杂田间环境下的实际贡献可能受到其他代谢途径的补偿或掩盖。
3. **遗传不稳定性**：由于 ZmTPS42 在许多玉米品系中存在假基因化，该防御途径在现代栽培品种中的普遍性受限。

title: Distinct Multimodal Imaging Correlates of Depression in Middle-Aged Adults With and Without a Family History of Alzheimer Disease
abstract: Background: Depression is associated with risk for late-onset Alzheimer&aposs disease (LOAD), but its underlying pathogenesis in at-risk individuals remains unclear. We examined multimodal imaging correlates of depressive symptoms in cognitively normal middle-aged offspring of patients with LOAD (O-LOAD) compared with control individuals without LOAD history up to a 4th degree of kinship (HC). Methods: Participants (n=58; 52{+/-}3 years; 74% female) underwent assessment with the Beck Depression Inventory II (BDI), structural MRI, resting-state fMRI, FDG-PET, and PiB-PET. Resting state fMRI data were available for 28 O-LOAD and 24 HC; PET data for 24 O-LOAD and 22 HC. General linear models tested associations between imaging measures and BDI, including group interactions. Results: In O-LOAD, higher BDI scores were associated with reduced cortical thickness in the left postcentral gyrus. Resting-state fMRI revealed significant group by BDI interactions involving cingulate and orbitofrontal networks. In O-LOAD, greater depressive symptom severity was associated with reduced cingulate connectivity across distributed corticolimbic, prefrontal, insular, occipital, and cerebellar regions ({beta} range - 0.10 to -0.18). In HC, depressive symptoms were associated with reduced right orbitofrontal and somatosensory-medial orbitofrontal connectivity ({beta}=-0.13), with divergent patterns of cingulate connectivity. FDG-PET showed no significant associations with depressive symptoms. PiB-PET demonstrated regionally specific associations between amyloid signal and BDI in HC, involving an inverse pattern in anterior and posterior insular cortices. Conclusions: Depressive symptoms in middle-aged individuals at familial risk for LOAD are associated with distinct structural and functional alterations, involving circuitry subserving salience and reward, and suggesting early network-level mechanisms linking affective symptoms with vulnerability to neurodegeneration.
abstract_zh: 抑郁与晚发型阿尔茨海默病（LOAD）风险密切相关，但在高风险人群中的发病机制尚不明确。本研究对比了认知正常的LOAD患者中年子女（O-LOAD，n=28）与无家族史的对照组（HC，n=24），探讨抑郁症状的多模态影像学相关性。受试者平均年龄52岁，通过BDI-II量表评估抑郁症状，并进行结构MRI、静息态fMRI、FDG-PET和PiB-PET扫描。结果显示，O-LOAD组BDI得分升高与左侧中央后回皮层厚度减少相关。fMRI分析发现，O-LOAD组抑郁症状加重与扣带回在皮层边缘、前额叶、岛叶及小脑等区域的连接性降低显著相关（β值-0.10至-0.18）；而HC组则表现为右侧眶额网络连接降低。PiB-PET显示HC组岛叶淀粉样蛋白信号与BDI存在区域特异性关联。研究表明，具有LOAD家族风险的中年人抑郁症状涉及独特的结构与功能网络改变，提示了情感症状与神经退行性易感性之间的早期连接机制。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏基础模型/Agent方向，可能影响病原检测任务的建模上限，值得关注其任务定义与评测设计。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究利用多模态成像技术（结构MRI、rs-fMRI、FDG-PET、PiB-PET）分析了具有阿尔茨海默病（LOAD）家族史的中年人群在认知正常阶段，其抑郁症状（BDI评分）与大脑结构及功能连接之间的关系。研究重点对比了有家族史（O-LOAD）与无家族史（HC）两组人群在神经影像学表现上的差异。

## 为什么相关
尽管分类标签标注为 `foundation_model_agent`，但从摘要内容看，该研究属于典型的**临床神经影像学**范畴。其与 AI/LLM 的相关性极弱，并未涉及基础模型或智能体架构。若要强行关联，其多模态数据的整合分析方法（如处理 MRI 和 PET 的融合数据）可为未来构建神经退行性疾病的预测大模型提供多维特征工程的参考。

## 新颖度判断
**新颖度：中等偏低（0.25）**。研究的新颖之处在于聚焦于“中年期”且“认知正常”的 LOAD 高危后代，试图寻找阿尔茨海默病临床前期的早期生物标志物。然而，所使用的统计方法（广义线性模型 GLM）和影像学指标（皮层厚度、功能连接、淀粉样蛋白沉积）属于该领域的常规手段，缺乏算法层面的突破。

## 方法与证据
- **实验对象**：58名中年受试者（平均52岁），其中 O-LOAD 组与 HC 组在 fMRI（n=52）和 PET（n=46）样本量上略有不同。
- **分析方法**：使用广义线性模型（GLM）测试影像学测量值与 BDI 评分的关联及组间交互作用。
- **关键证据**：在 O-LOAD 组中观察到扣带回连接性广泛下降（β 范围 -0.10 至 -0.18），涉及前额叶和岛叶等区域；而在 HC 组中，抑郁主要与右侧眶额和体感-内侧眶额连接降低相关。PiB-PET 仅在 HC 组中发现岛叶淀粉样蛋白与抑郁评分的相关性。

## 应用价值
该研究识别了 LOAD 高风险人群特有的神经回路改变（如扣带回和眶额网络），这有助于在临床前阶段识别抑郁症状背后的神经退行性风险。这些发现可作为早期干预的潜在靶点，或用于优化阿尔茨海默病风险预测模型的特征选择。

## 局限与风险
- **样本量限制**：总样本量仅 58 人，亚组分析（如 PET 仅 46 人）可能导致统计效能不足，结论的普适性有待验证。
- **AI 深度不足**：摘要未提及任何机器学习或深度学习模型的应用，仅使用了传统的统计推断，无法体现 AI 在复杂多模态数据挖掘中的优势。
- **因果关系不明**：作为横断面研究，无法确定抑郁症状是神经退行性改变的原因还是结果。

title: Mamba-SSM with LLM Reasoning for Feature Selection: Faithfulness-Aware Biomarker Discovery
abstract: Gradient saliency from deep sequence models surfaces candidate biomarkers efficiently, but the resulting gene lists can be contaminated by tissue-composition confounders that degrade downstream classifiers. We study whether LLM chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning can filter these confounders, and whether reasoning quality is associated with downstream performance. We train a Mamba SSM on TCGA-BRCA RNA-seq and extract the top-50 genes by gradient saliency; DeepSeek-R1 evaluates every candidate with structured CoT to produce a final 17-gene set. On the held-out test split, the raw 50-gene saliency set (no LLM) performs worse than a 5,000-gene variance baseline (AUC 0.832 vs. 0.903), while the LLM-filtered set surpasses it (AUC 0.927), using 294x fewer features. A faithfulness audit (COSMIC CGC, OncoKB, PAM50) shows that 6 of 17 selected genes (35.3%) are validated BRCA biomarkers, while 10 of 16 known BRCA genes present in the input were missed - including FOXA1. This divergence between downstream performance and reasoning faithfulness suggests selective faithfulness in this setting: targeted confounder removal can improve predictive performance without comprehensive recall.
abstract_zh: 深度序列模型的梯度显著性可高效提取候选生物标志物，但结果常受组织成分等混杂因素干扰，降低下游分类器性能。本研究探讨了大语言模型（LLM）思维链（CoT）推理能否过滤这些混杂因素，以及推理质量与下游性能的关系。研究者在 TCGA-BRCA RNA-seq 数据集上训练了 Mamba 状态空间模型（SSM），提取梯度显著性最高的前 50 个基因，并利用 DeepSeek-R1 通过结构化 CoT 筛选出 17 个基因。实验结果显示，在独立测试集上，原始 50 基因集（无 LLM 过滤）的 AUC 为 0.832，低于 5000 基因方差基线的 0.903；而 LLM 过滤后的 17 基因集 AUC 提升至 0.927，且特征数减少了 294 倍。忠实度审计显示，17 个基因中有 6 个（35.3%）被 COSMIC 等数据库验证，但遗漏了 16 个已知 BRCA 基因中的 10 个（如 FOXA1）。这种预测性能与推理忠实度的分歧表明，针对性的混杂因素去除可在不具备全面召回率的情况下显著提升预测性能。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏基础模型/Agent方向，可能影响病原检测任务的建模上限，值得关注其任务定义与评测设计。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究提出了一种结合 Mamba 状态空间模型（SSM）与大语言模型（LLM）推理的生物标志物发现框架。利用 Mamba 模型从 TCGA-BRCA（乳腺癌）RNA-seq 数据中提取初步的梯度显著性基因，随后引入 DeepSeek-R1 模型通过思维链（CoT）推理对候选基因进行筛选，旨在剔除影响分类性能的组织成分混杂因素，并评估 LLM 推理的忠实度与预测准确性之间的关系。

## 为什么相关
本研究属于基因组学与 AI/LLM 的强交叉领域。它解决了生物信息学中一个关键痛点：深度学习模型提取的特征往往包含大量噪声或生物学无关的混杂因素。对于病原检测或肿瘤诊断而言，如何利用 LLM 的先验知识库来增强黑盒模型（如 Mamba）的可解释性并优化特征选择，是当前 AI for Science 的前沿方向。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度较高。研究不仅应用了高性能的 Mamba-SSM 架构处理序列数据，更首次系统性地引入 DeepSeek-R1 的结构化 CoT 推理作为生物特征的“过滤器”。此外，论文提出了“选择性忠实度（Selective Faithfulness）”的概念，即 LLM 即使无法找回所有已知标志物，只要能有效剔除混杂因素，也能显著提升下游任务性能。

## 方法与证据
- **数据与模型**：使用 TCGA-BRCA RNA-seq 数据，训练 Mamba SSM 模型。
- **筛选流程**：从 Mamba 提取 top-50 显著基因，经 DeepSeek-R1 过滤至 17 个基因。
- **性能指标**：LLM 过滤后的 17 基因集 AUC（0.927）显著优于原始 50 基因集（0.832）和 5000 基因方差基线（0.903）。
- **验证工具**：使用 COSMIC CGC、OncoKB 和 PAM50 数据库进行忠实度审计，证实 35.3% 的筛选基因具有已知生物学意义。

## 应用价值
该方法具有极高的临床转化潜力。通过将特征规模缩小 294 倍（从 5000 降至 17）同时提升预测准确率，可以极大地降低临床检测的成本（如开发更精简的 PCR Panel）。这种“AI 提炼+LLM 专家知识过滤”的模式可推广至病原体耐药基因筛选或罕见病诊断特征提取。

## 局限与风险
- **召回率不足**：摘要明确指出 LLM 遗漏了 16 个已知 BRCA 基因中的 10 个（如关键的 FOXA1），说明 LLM 过滤可能存在过度筛选或知识盲区。
- **知识滞后性**：LLM 的推理依赖于其预训练语料，对于最新发现的、尚未进入数据库的生物标志物可能存在偏见。
- **计算成本**：虽然特征数减少，但引入 DeepSeek-R1 的 CoT 推理增加了推理阶段的计算开销。

title: A Case Study on the Impact of Anonymization Along the RAG Pipeline
abstract: Despite the considerable promise of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), many real-world use cases may create privacy concerns, where the purported utility of RAG-enabled insights comes at the risk of exposing private information to either the LLM or the end user requesting the response. As a potential mitigation, using anonymization techniques to remove personally identifiable information (PII) and other sensitive markers in the underlying data represents a practical and sensible course of action for RAG administrators. Despite a wealth of literature on the topic, no works consider the placement of anonymization along the RAG pipeline, i.e., asking the question, where should anonymization happen? In this case study, we systematically and empirically measure the impact of anonymization at two important points along the RAG pipeline: the dataset and generated answer. We show that differences in privacy-utility trade-offs can be observed depending on where anonymization took place, demonstrating the significance of privacy risk mitigation placement in RAG.
abstract_zh: 尽管检索增强生成（RAG）展现出巨大潜力，但在实际应用中常引发隐私担忧，即 RAG 驱动的见解可能向大语言模型（LLM）或终端用户泄露私密信息。作为缓解措施，RAG 管理员通常采用匿名化技术移除底层数据中的个人身份信息（PII）及其他敏感标记。尽管已有大量相关文献，但尚未有研究探讨匿名化在 RAG 流水线中的具体位置，即“匿名化应该发生在何处？”。本案例研究系统地、实证地衡量了在 RAG 流水线的两个关键点——数据集和生成答案——进行匿名化的影响。研究结果表明，隐私与效用之间的权衡会因匿名化发生的位置不同而产生显著差异，这证明了在 RAG 架构中合理安置隐私风险缓解措施具有重要意义。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏基础模型/Agent方向，可能影响病原检测任务的建模上限，值得关注其任务定义与评测设计。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究聚焦于检索增强生成（RAG）流水线中的隐私保护问题，重点探讨了匿名化技术（Anonymization）在不同阶段实施对系统性能和隐私泄露风险的影响。研究对比了在“底层数据集”阶段和“最终生成答案”阶段进行脱敏处理的差异。

## 为什么相关
在病原生物学和临床微生物学领域，构建基于 RAG 的 AI Agent（如临床决策支持系统或基因组关联分析助手）时，不可避免地会处理包含患者隐私、地理位置或敏感临床表型的底层数据。本文探讨的 RAG 流水线匿名化位置问题，直接关系到如何在保护患者隐私（PII）的同时，确保 AI 提取的病原学见解或诊断建议的准确性，是临床 AI 落地必须解决的基础架构问题。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度评分为 6.0（中等偏高）。其创新点在于填补了 RAG 流程研究的一个空白：以往研究多关注匿名化算法本身，而本文首次系统性地提出了“匿名化位置”这一变量，并实证研究了其对“隐私-效用权衡”（Privacy-Utility Trade-offs）的影响。

## 方法与证据
- **实验对象**：RAG 流水线中的两个关键节点：数据集（Dataset）和生成的答案（Generated Answer）。
- **研究方法**：采用案例研究法（Case Study），通过系统性的实证测量来评估影响。
- **关键证据**：摘要指出，在不同位置实施匿名化会导致隐私保护效果与模型效用（Utility）之间的权衡关系出现观测到的差异。摘要未说明具体使用的 LLM 模型名称、数据集规模或具体的 PII 识别算法。

## 应用价值
对于开发临床微生物学 AI 工具的工程师和管理员具有指导意义。它提示开发者不能简单地在流水线末端进行脱敏，而应根据业务对“效用”和“隐私”的敏感程度，决定是在向量数据库索引前（数据集端）还是在向用户展示前（生成端）进行匿名化，以优化 RAG 系统的整体表现。

## 局限与风险
- **弱 AI 交叉**：该工作属于基础模型架构与隐私工程的交叉，并非直接针对生物医学数据设计的算法，其结论在高度专业化的病原基因组数据上的适用性仍需验证。
- **细节缺失**：摘要未说明具体的评估指标（如准确率下降百分比或隐私泄露率的具体数值），也未提及是否涉及多模态数据（如临床影像或序列数据）的脱敏，仅提到了 PII。

title: TPA: Next Token Probability Attribution for Detecting Hallucinations in RAG
abstract: Detecting hallucinations in Retrieval-Augmented Generation remains a challenge. Prior approaches attribute hallucinations to a binary conflict between internal knowledge stored in FFNs and the retrieved context. However, this perspective is incomplete, failing to account for the impact of other components of the LLM, such as the user query, previously generated tokens, the self token, and the final LayerNorm adjustment. To comprehensively capture the impact of these components on hallucination detection, we propose TPA which mathematically attributes each token's probability to seven distinct sources: Query, RAG Context, Past Token, Self Token, FFN, Final LayerNorm, and Initial Embedding. This attribution quantifies how each source contributes to the generation of the next token. Specifically, we aggregate these attribution scores by Part-of-Speech (POS) tags to quantify the contribution of each model component to the generation of specific linguistic categories within a response. By leveraging these patterns, such as detecting anomalies where Nouns rely heavily on LayerNorm, TPA effectively identifies hallucinated responses. Extensive experiments show that TPA achieves state-of-the-art performance.
abstract_zh: 在检索增强生成（RAG）中检测幻觉仍是一项挑战。先前的方法通常将幻觉归因于前馈网络（FFN）中存储的内部知识与检索上下文之间的二元冲突。然而，这种视角并不完整，未能考虑大语言模型（LLM）其他组件（如用户查询、历史生成词、自身词及最终层归一化 LayerNorm 调整）的影响。为全面捕捉这些组件对幻觉检测的作用，我们提出了 TPA 方法，从数学上将每个词的概率归因于七个不同来源：查询、RAG 上下文、历史词、自身词、FFN、最终 LayerNorm 和初始嵌入。该方法量化了各来源对下个词生成的贡献。具体而言，我们按词性（POS）标签聚合归因分数，以量化模型组件对响应中特定语言类别的贡献。通过识别异常模式（例如名词生成高度依赖 LayerNorm），TPA 能有效识别幻觉响应。实验表明，TPA 达到了当前最先进的性能。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏基础模型/Agent方向，可能影响病原检测任务的建模上限，值得关注其任务定义与评测设计。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究提出了一种名为 TPA（Next Token Probability Attribution）的幻觉检测方法，专门用于检索增强生成（RAG）场景。其核心逻辑是将 LLM 生成下一个词的概率分解为七个维度的数学归因，并通过词性（POS）聚合分析来识别模型何时在“胡编乱造”。

## 为什么相关
在病原生物学和临床微生物学领域，研究人员常利用 RAG 技术构建知识库问答系统（如查询耐药基因数据库或临床指南）。幻觉（Hallucination）可能导致错误的诊断建议或错误的病原体分类。TPA 提供了一种底层解释工具，能够监控模型输出是否真正基于“RAG 上下文”，还是受内部偏见或 LayerNorm 异常驱动，从而提升临床决策支持系统的可靠性。该工作属于 AI 基础架构与下游任务的交叉，相关度为中等偏弱，但对构建高可信度生物信息 Agent 具有参考价值。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度较高（5.5）。相比于以往仅关注“内部知识 vs. 外部上下文”的二元对立，TPA 首次将归因细化到七个来源，并引入了“最终 LayerNorm”和“词性（POS）”作为幻觉检测的关键特征。这种从模型内部动力学出发的细粒度分解在 RAG 幻觉研究中具有开创性。

## 方法与证据
- **方法**：TPA 将概率归因于：Query（查询）、RAG Context（上下文）、Past Token（历史词）、Self Token（自身词）、FFN（前馈网络）、Final LayerNorm（最终层归一化）和 Initial Embedding（初始嵌入）。
- **特征提取**：利用词性标注（POS tags）聚合分数。摘要提到一个关键证据模式：当“名词（Nouns）”的生成高度依赖于“LayerNorm”而非“RAG Context”时，通常预示着幻觉的发生。
- **实验结果**：摘要声称在广泛实验中达到了 SOTA（最先进）水平，但未列出具体的数据集名称或 F1 分数等硬指标。

## 应用价值
- **诊断安全**：可集成至临床微生物 AI 助手，当模型生成的关键术语（如细菌名称、药物剂量）表现出异常归因模式时，自动触发预警。
- **模型调试**：帮助开发者理解 RAG 系统失效的原因，是检索内容未被利用，还是模型受历史对话干扰过大。

## 局限与风险
- **计算开销**：摘要未说明对七个来源进行数学归因的计算成本，在实时临床问答中可能存在延迟风险。
- **词性依赖**：该方法依赖于词性标注的准确性，对于包含大量复杂生物医学专有名词（如 *Acinetobacter baumannii*）的文本，通用的 POS 工具可能失效。
- **泛化性未知**：摘要未说明该方法在不同规模（如 7B vs 70B）或不同架构（如 MoE）模型上的表现差异。

title: RepoShapley: Shapley-Enhanced Context Filtering for Repository-Level Code Completion
abstract: Repository-level code completion benefits from retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). However, controlling cross-file evidence is difficult because chunk utility is often interaction-dependent: some snippets help only when paired with complementary context, while others harm decoding when they conflict. We propose RepoShapley, a coalition-aware context filtering framework supervised by Shapley-style marginal contributions. Our offline labeling module, ChunkShapley, estimates signed per-chunk effects via teacher-forced probing, feeds them into a lightweight surrogate game that captures saturation and interference, computes exact Shapley values for small retrieval sets, and selects a decoding-optimal coalition through bounded post-verification with the frozen generator. The verified keep/drop decisions and retrieval triggers are then distilled into a single model via discrete control tokens. Experiments across benchmarks and backbones show that RepoShapley improves completion quality while reducing harmful context and unnecessary retrieval.
abstract_zh: 仓库级代码补全受益于检索增强生成（RAG），但跨文件证据的控制具有挑战性，因为代码块的效用往往依赖于交互：某些片段仅在与互补上下文配对时才有帮助，而另一些在冲突时会损害解码。我们提出 RepoShapley，这是一个由 Shapley 风格边际贡献监督的联盟感知上下文过滤框架。其离线标注模块 ChunkShapley 通过教师强制探测（teacher-forced probing）估计每个代码块的正负效应，并将其输入到一个捕捉饱和与干扰的轻量级代理博弈模型中，为小型检索集计算精确的 Shapley 值，并通过冻结生成器的有界后验证选择解码最优联盟。随后，验证后的保留/舍弃决策和检索触发器通过离散控制令牌蒸馏到单个模型中。在不同基准和骨干模型上的实验表明，RepoShapley 在提高补全质量的同时，减少了有害上下文和不必要的检索。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更像方法创新，可能直接关联 metagenomics、long-read 或 pathogen identification 流程优化。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究提出了 RepoShapley 框架，旨在解决仓库级代码补全中 RAG 检索内容的质量控制问题。核心在于引入了博弈论中的 Shapley 值来量化不同检索块（chunks）之间的交互效应（协同或干扰），并通过 ChunkShapley 模块进行离线标注和模型蒸馏，实现更精准的上下文过滤和检索触发控制。

## 为什么相关
本研究属于 AI 方法论创新。虽然直接对象是代码补全，但其核心逻辑——**在复杂上下文中识别具有正向贡献的片段并排除干扰项**——与病原基因组学中的长读段拼接、宏基因组多源数据融合以及临床微生物决策支持系统中的 RAG 优化高度相似。在处理复杂的病原体元数据或多序列比对时，如何剔除相互冲突的“噪声”证据是提升模型准确性的关键。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度较高（5.75）。不同于传统的基于相似度的检索过滤，RepoShapley 关注“联盟感知”（coalition-aware），即考虑了信息块之间的相互作用。利用 Shapley 值来处理 RAG 中的上下文饱和与干扰问题，并将其蒸馏为离散控制令牌，在处理长上下文依赖任务中具有较强的技术先进性。

## 方法与证据
1. **ChunkShapley 模块**：利用教师强制探测（teacher-forced probing）估计每个块的边际贡献。
2. **代理博弈模型**：用于捕捉信息块之间的饱和（冗余）和干扰效应，并在小型检索集上计算精确 Shapley 值。
3. **后验证机制**：通过冻结的生成器进行有界验证，确保选出的块联盟是解码最优的。
4. **实验证据**：摘要提到在多个基准测试（benchmarks）和骨干模型（backbones）上进行了实验，结果显示 RepoShapley 提升了补全质量并减少了不必要的检索，但摘要未列出具体的百分比指标。

## 应用价值
在生物信息学领域，该方法可转化为：
1. **宏基因组 RAG 系统**：在利用大模型进行病原体鉴定或耐药基因分析时，过滤掉与当前样本特征冲突的参考序列片段。
2. **长读段序列分析**：在处理高噪声的纳米孔测序数据时，识别哪些序列片段对最终的一致性序列（consensus）有正向贡献。

## 局限与风险
1. **计算开销**：尽管使用了代理模型，但 Shapley 值的计算在检索集增大时仍面临组合爆炸问题，摘要明确指出其适用于“小型检索集”。
2. **弱 AI 交叉**：该论文完全基于代码补全任务，并未在生物序列或临床数据上验证，迁移到病原分析领域需要重新定义“代码块”与“解码效用”的对应关系。
3. **离线依赖**：标注过程依赖离线模块，可能无法实时处理极大规模的动态数据库。

title: VRAG-DFD: Verifiable Retrieval-Augmentation for MLLM-based Deepfake Detection
abstract: In Deepfake Detection (DFD) tasks, researchers proposed two types of MLLM-based methods: complementary combination with small DFD detectors, or static forgery knowledge injection. The lack of professional forgery knowledge hinders the performance of these DFD-MLLMs. To solve this, we deeply considered two insightful issues: How to provide high-quality associated forgery knowledge for MLLMs? AND How to endow MLLMs with critical reasoning abilities given noisy reference information? Notably, we attempted to address above two questions with preliminary answers by leveraging the combination of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and Reinforcement Learning (RL). Through RAG and RL techniques, we propose the VRAG-DFD framework with accurate dynamic forgery knowledge retrieval and powerful critical reasoning capabilities. Specifically, in terms of data, we constructed two datasets with RAG: Forensic Knowledge Database (FKD) for DFD knowledge annotation, and Forensic Chain-of-Thought Dataset (F-CoT), for critical CoT construction. In terms of model training, we adopt a three-stage training method (Alignment->SFT->GRPO) to gradually cultivate the critical reasoning ability of the MLLM. In terms of performance, VRAG-DFD achieved SOTA and competitive performance on DFD generalization testing.
abstract_zh: 在深度伪造检测（DFD）任务中，现有基于多模态大语言模型（MLLM）的方法主要依赖于与小型检测器的互补结合或静态伪造知识注入，缺乏专业伪造知识限制了其性能。为解决此问题，本研究提出了VRAG-DFD框架，通过结合检索增强生成（RAG）与强化学习（RL），为模型提供高质量动态伪造知识并赋予其批判性推理能力。在数据层面，研究构建了用于知识标注的取证知识库（FKD）和用于批判性思维链构建的取证思维链数据集（F-CoT）。在模型训练上，采用“对齐-指令微调（SFT）-GRPO（群体相对策略优化）”三阶段方法，逐步培养模型的推理能力。实验结果显示，VRAG-DFD在DFD泛化测试中达到了SOTA及具有竞争力的性能水平。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏数据集或基准构建，适合判断是否能作为病原组学训练或评测资源。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究提出了一个名为 VRAG-DFD 的可验证检索增强框架，旨在提升多模态大语言模型（MLLM）在深度伪造检测（DFD）中的表现。核心组件包括两个新数据集：取证知识库（FKD）和取证思维链数据集（F-CoT），以及一个结合了 RAG 和强化学习（特别是 GRPO 算法）的三阶段训练流程（Alignment -> SFT -> GRPO）。

## 为什么相关
本论文与病原生信或临床微生物学的直接相关性较弱，属于**弱 AI 交叉**。其相关性体现在方法论的迁移潜力：在临床微生物鉴定或病原基因组异常检测中，同样面临“专业知识缺乏”和“需要批判性推理”的问题。VRAG-DFD 处理多模态取证证据的逻辑，可类比于在临床诊断中结合测序数据（多模态输入）与已知病原数据库（RAG 检索）进行逻辑推理的过程。

## 新颖度判断
**新颖度较高（5.75）**。该工作首次将 RAG 与强化学习中的 GRPO 算法结合应用于深度伪造检测领域。相比于传统的静态知识注入，其动态检索和三阶段训练法（尤其是引入批判性推理的 CoT）在处理噪声参考信息方面具有显著的创新性。

## 方法与证据
- **数据规模与构建**：构建了 FKD 用于存储专业伪造特征知识，以及 F-CoT 用于训练模型生成逻辑严密的推理链。摘要未说明具体样本条数。
- **模型架构**：基于 MLLM，引入 RAG 机制动态检索 FKD 中的知识。
- **训练策略**：采用三阶段法，重点在于第三阶段使用 GRPO（Group Relative Policy Optimization）强化学习算法，优化模型在面对噪声干扰时的批判性推理能力。
- **实验结论**：在 DFD 泛化性测试中达到 SOTA 性能，证明了框架在未见过的伪造类型上的有效性。

## 应用价值
在生物信息学领域，该框架的逻辑可用于构建“可验证的病原鉴定系统”。例如，利用 RAG 检索最新的耐药基因数据库，并通过类 F-CoT 的推理链解释为何某段序列被判定为高致病性，提高 AI 在临床决策中的可信度与透明度。

## 局限与风险
- **领域局限性**：实验对象仅限于图像/视频的深度伪造检测，未在基因组序列或蛋白质结构等生物数据上验证。
- **计算成本**：三阶段训练（尤其是 RL 阶段）和动态 RAG 检索可能带来较高的计算开销。
- **数据依赖**：性能高度依赖于 FKD 知识库的质量和 F-CoT 标注的准确性，摘要未提及如何处理知识库本身的错误信息。

title: VoxMind: An End-to-End Agentic Spoken Dialogue System
abstract: Recent end-to-end spoken dialogue models enable natural interaction. However, as user demands become increasingly complex, models that rely solely on conversational abilities often struggle to cope. Incorporating agentic capabilities is therefore essential: by enabling tool use, these models can extend their knowledge boundaries and better solve real-world tasks. Yet, existing research has largely concentrated on core perception and generation, with comparatively limited exploration of such tool-augmented extensions. To bridge this gap, we present VoxMind, an integrated framework designed to equip end-to-end spoken dialogue models with comprehensive agentic abilities. Leveraging our curated 470-hour AgentChat dataset, we incorporate a "Think-before-Speak" mechanism, enabling the model to internalize structured reasoning as a critical prerequisite for planning and response generation. Furthermore, to mitigate latency bottlenecks caused by large-scale tool integration, we propose a Multi-Agent Dynamic Tool Management architecture. By asynchronously delegating retrieval tasks to an auxiliary agent aligned with the main model's reasoning trajectory, this system effectively decouples inference latency from toolset size. Experimental results confirm that VoxMind achieves significant improvements in agent performance: compared with strong baselines, the task completion rate increases from 34.88% to 74.57%, outperforming Gemini-2.5-Pro on spoken agent tasks while preserving general conversational quality. The source code and associated data are publicly available at https://github.com/MM-Speech/VoxMind.
abstract_zh: 现有的端到端语音对话模型在处理复杂用户需求时，往往受限于单一的对话能力。为此，本文提出 VoxMind 框架，旨在赋予端到端语音对话模型全面的智能体（Agent）能力。研究团队构建了包含 470 小时的 AgentChat 数据集，并引入“先思后言”（Think-before-Speak）机制，使模型能够将结构化推理内化为规划与响应的关键前提。针对大规模工具集成导致的延迟瓶颈，VoxMind 采用了多智能体动态工具管理架构，通过辅助智能体异步处理检索任务，实现了推理延迟与工具集规模的解耦。实验结果表明，VoxMind 在语音 Agent 任务中的表现显著优于强基准模型，任务完成率从 34.88% 提升至 74.57%，在保持通用对话质量的同时，性能超越了 Gemini-2.5-Pro。目前，源代码与数据集已公开。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏基础模型/Agent方向，可能影响病原检测任务的建模上限，值得关注其任务定义与评测设计。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
VoxMind 是一个端到端的语音对话 Agent 框架，核心在于通过“先思后言”机制和多智能体架构解决语音模型在复杂任务中调用工具的效率与准确性问题。其核心组件包括 470 小时的 AgentChat 数据集、结构化推理内化机制以及异步工具管理系统。

## 为什么相关
该研究与病原生信及临床微生物学的相关度属于**弱 AI 交叉**。虽然 VoxMind 是通用语音 Agent 框架，但其“工具调用”和“异步检索”能力对于临床实验室场景具有潜在价值。例如，在生物安全实验室（BSL-3/4）等不便操作电脑的环境下，研究员可通过此类端到端语音 Agent 调取病原体基因组数据库或操作自动化测序仪。其任务定义与评测设计可为构建临床微生物专用语音助手提供架构参考。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度较高（5.75）。其创新点在于：1. 首次在端到端语音模型中系统性引入“Think-before-Speak”推理机制；2. 提出了多智能体动态工具管理架构，解决了语音 Agent 在面对海量工具（如复杂的生信分析工具链）时常见的推理延迟问题。

## 方法与证据
- **数据规模**：使用了专门策划的 470 小时 AgentChat 数据集进行训练。
- **核心机制**：引入“Think-before-Speak”，要求模型在生成语音响应前先进行结构化推理规划。
- **架构设计**：采用多智能体动态工具管理，将检索任务异步委托给辅助智能体，使推理延迟不再随工具数量增加而线性增长。
- **实验结论**：在语音 Agent 任务中，任务完成率（Task Completion Rate）从基准的 34.88% 大幅提升至 74.57%，在对比实验中击败了 Gemini-2.5-Pro。

## 应用价值
在临床微生物学领域，该技术可应用于：1. **无接触实验室交互**：通过语音指令控制生信分析流程或查询临床微生物检测报告；2. **复杂任务规划**：利用其推理机制，辅助医生进行多步骤的病原诊断决策支持；3. **高效工具集成**：其异步管理架构允许集成数百个专门的生信分析 API 而不显著增加语音响应延迟。

## 局限与风险
- **领域知识缺失**：摘要未说明该模型在医学或生物信息学等专业领域的术语理解准确率，通用数据集可能无法覆盖复杂的病原体命名法。
- **安全性风险**：在临床决策中，语音 Agent 的“推理”过程若出现幻觉，可能导致错误的实验操作或诊断建议。
- **实时性挑战**：虽然解耦了工具规模与延迟，但端到端语音推理本身的计算开销在摘要中未给出具体毫秒级数据。
